back: A language used to write instructions that a computer can follow.
A programming language that is easy for humans to read and write using English-like commands.

front: Give an example of a high-level language.
back: Python.

front: Give one advantage of high-level languages.
back: They are easier to read, write and debug.

front: Give one disadvantage of high-level languages.
back: They are slower and give less direct control of hardware.
A language close to machine code that is designed for the CPU.
Machine code and assembly language.

front: Give one advantage of low-level languages.
back: They give direct control of hardware and run very fast.

front: Give one disadvantage of low-level languages.
back: For humans they are difficult to read, write and debug.
The binary instructions (1s and 0s) that the CPU understands directly.
The part of machine code that represents the instruction.
The part of machine code that represents data or memory locations.

front: In machine code 01110 11011, which part is the opcode and which part is the operand?
back:
01110 = opcode (instruction)
11011 = operand (data or memory location)

front: What do opcodes and operands represent in machine code?
back:
Opcode = instruction to perform
Operand = data or memory location used
A low-level language that uses mnemonics instead of binary.
A short code that represents a machine instruction, such as ADD or LDA.
Software that converts code from one language into another.

front: Why must all programs be translated?
back: Because the CPU can only understand machine code.
A translator that converts assembly language into machine code.
A translator that converts the entire program into machine code at once.
An executable file.

front: When are errors reported in a compiled program?
back: After the whole program has been compiled.
A translator that converts and executes code line by line.
When an error is found.

front: Give one advantage of an interpreter.
back: It is useful for development and debugging.

front: Give one disadvantage of an interpreter.
back: Programs run slower because they are translated line by line.

front: What is the key difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
back: A compiler translates the whole program at once, while an interpreter translates line by line.

front: Why is a compiler used for final programs?
back: Because compiled programs run faster and the source code is not visible.
